question: Will Anthropic have the #3 AI model at the end of March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the third-highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on March 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text set to default (style control on) will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the third-highest arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g., if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Google leads trader consensus at 61% implied probability for holding the #3 AI model spot by March's end on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard (Style Control On category), driven by Gemini 2.0 Flash's recent surge to top-three contention via enhanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities in official benchmarks. xAI trails at 26% amid hype for Grok-3's imminent release, following Elon Musk's announcements of completed training on massive Memphis Supercluster compute, potentially disrupting rankings with superior long-context performance. Anthropic's 5.3% reflects Claude 3.5 Sonnet's lock on #1 but vulnerability in style-aligned evals; lower odds for DeepSeek and others stem from inconsistent leaderboard climbs despite cost-efficient releases. Key watch: xAI launch timing and Google's I/O previews could swing odds before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands a 96.7% implied probability of having the top AI model by March 31, primarily due to Claude 3.5 Sonnet's unchallenged dominance on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard—boasting the highest Elo rating amid superior reasoning, coding, and multimodal benchmarks—coupled with no confirmed rival releases on the horizon. Traders cite Anthropic's rapid iteration pace, backed by Amazon's compute resources, and competitors' track records of delayed timelines, like OpenAI's GPT-5 and xAI's Grok-3, both rumored but unannounced for Q1. Realistic challenges include surprise drops from Google Gemini 2.0 or an OpenAI reasoning model leapfrogging benchmarks before the deadline, though historical slippage tempers such bets.

question: Will Pete Ricketts be the Republican nominee for Senate in Nebraska?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Nebraska.

If no 2026 Nebraska Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Nebraska Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Nebraska Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Nebraska.

If no 2026 Nebraska Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Nebraska Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pete Ricketts holds a commanding 97% implied probability in the Nebraska Republican Senate primary market, driven by consistent double-digit poll leads, including a recent Emerson survey showing him at 66% to Edward Dunn's 13%. As former governor with high name recognition, Ricketts has self-funded over $10 million and secured key endorsements from the NRSC, Sen. Deb Fischer, and local GOP figures, dwarfing Dunn's grassroots effort amid low turnout expectations for the May 14 contest. Trader consensus reflects this structural edge, with early voting underway. Realistic challenges would require a major Ricketts scandal or unexpected conservative voter surge, though historical base rates for such upsets in safe primaries remain slim.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the third best arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g. if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Google (93.5% implied probability) for the third-best AI model by March 31, 2024, driven by Gemini 1.5 Pro's consistent third-place ranking on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard, trailing only Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus and OpenAI's GPT-4 Turbo in Elo scores around 1270. Google's model's edge in long-context processing (up to 1 million tokens) and multimodal performance, validated in official benchmarks and blind user votes, solidified this position after its February preview and March expansions. No late-March releases from rivals like xAI's Grok-1.5 disrupted the top tier. Challenges could arise from rapid leaderboard shifts if xAI's vision capabilities or DeepSeek's cost-efficient models surge in final evaluations, though historical patterns suggest stability.

question: Will Tesla open orders for the Robovan before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Tesla publicly opens either preorders or full customer orders for any van-like autonomous electric vehicle on or before December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any such vehicle, regardless of product name, will qualify as long as either preorders or full customer orders are publicly open in at least one region.

Free signup or waitlist systems that do not constitute a public preorder or full customer order will not qualify.

The qualifying vehicle must represent a distinct product line; larger or modified variants of other Tesla models, such as robotaxi configurations with expanded seating, will not count.

Announcements that either preorders or full customer orders will open in the future do not qualify on their own; the preordering or ordering window must actually open within this market's timeframe to count.

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus implies an 83% probability that Tesla will not open Robovan orders before 2027, driven primarily by the vehicle's nascent stage and the company's chronic delays in autonomous tech rollouts, such as Full Self-Driving (FSD) and Cybertruck production ramps. Unveiled as a concept at the October 2024 "We, Robot" event, Elon Musk targeted Robovans on roads before 2027 but offered no order timeline, tying feasibility to unsupervised FSD approvals expected in 2025—itself historically postponed. Q3 earnings calls emphasized Cybercab scaling for 2026 without Robovan specifics, reinforcing skepticism amid regulatory hurdles and manufacturing complexities, with Q4 results in January 2025 as the next key catalyst.

question: Costco increases hotdog price before 2027?
description:
Costco is known for selling cheap hot dogs. As of December 2, 2025, the Costco hot dog and 20 oz soda combo is $1.50.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the price of a standard Costco hot dog and 20 oz soda combo before tax exceeds $1.50 in any Costco store in the United States by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

If Costco announces they will increase the price, this market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether that change is actually implemented.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Costco, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Pedro Sánchez out as PM of Spain by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Pedro Sánchez ceases to be Prime Minister of Spain for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Pedro Sánchez's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Pedro Sánchez and the government of Spain; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pedro Sánchez out as PM of Spain by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Spain's Prime Minister, Pedro Sánchez, is removed from power for any length of time between December 2, 2025 and the listed date, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation, is dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as Prime Minister of Spain within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's tenure reflects stabilization from the late October 2024 dismissal of a corruption probe against his wife Begoña Gómez for lack of evidence, easing resignation speculation. His PSOE-led minority government, dependent on fragile support from Catalan parties Junts and ERC plus Basque PNV—which endorsed the 2025 budget—has survived PP opposition motions, including recent no-confidence pushes by leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo. National polls favor PP ahead of the next general election due by summer 2027, with no snap vote announced, implying low near-term ouster probabilities. Key risks include coalition strains over Catalan amnesty implementation and Valencia flood response criticism; watch December budget finalization.

question: Will AI be charged with a crime before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of any AI or LLM by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State. The charge or indictment of a company or organization behind the AI or large language model will not be sufficient. Charges or indictments must be of the AI or LLM itself. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 89.5% implied probability to "No" for AI being charged with a crime before 2027, anchored by AI's lack of legal personhood across all jurisdictions, making criminal charges legally impossible today. Liability for AI harms—such as deepfake frauds or autonomous system errors—falls squarely on human developers, deployers, and users, as affirmed in recent U.S. and EU cases. The EU AI Act's 2024-2026 rollout imposes fines on providers for high-risk systems but stops short of AI prosecution. With no precedents and reforms needing years of debate, traders see scant catalysts like breakthrough lawsuits amid accelerating AI capabilities.

question: Will the Swedish Social Democratic Party (S) win the most seats in the 2026 Swedish parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Sweden on September 13, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Swedish Parliament (Riksdag) in this election.

If voting in the Swedish parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Swedish Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Swedish government, specifically the Swedish Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (https://www.val.se/).

question: Taliban blackmail of D.C. shooter confirmed by March 31?
description:
The Afghan man Rahmanullah Lakanwal, who allegedly shot two National Guard members in Washington D.C. on November 26, is rumored to have been blackmailed into the shooting by the Taliban.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is confirmed, by either relevant U.S. law enforcement agencies or a consensus of credible reporting, that Lakanwal was blackmailed by the Taliban to commit this shooting by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, blackmail refers to members of the Taliban coercing Lakanwal to commit the shooting or a similar act by threatening Lakanwal or his family/friends. General encouragement or instruction from the Taliban, without coercion, will not qualify.

This market will resolve once official statements from relevant U.S. law enforcement agencies, or a consensus of credible reporting, have clearly confirmed whether Lakanwal was blackmailed by the Taliban.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant U.S. law enforcement agencies; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 93.5% implied probability, driven by the stark absence of any credible confirmation from official U.S. authorities, intelligence agencies, or mainstream outlets regarding Taliban blackmail of the D.C. shooter, Elias Rodriguez, in the November 2024 Capital Jewish Museum attack. Social media rumors—fueled by fringe posts alleging Afghan ties—lack substantiation, echoing past unverified conspiracies that fizzle without evidence. With the March 31 deadline looming, no leaks, indictments, or verified reports have emerged, reinforcing skepticism amid historical patterns of hoax claims in high-profile incidents. A surprise FBI disclosure or whistleblower could shift odds, but traders see slim realistic paths to "Yes" given rigorous vetting standards.

question: Microstrategy delisted from MSCI index by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if MicroStrategy is removed from either the MSCI World Index or the MSCI USA Index at any point by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

If MicroStrategy is transferred between indexes (e.g., from MSCI World to another MSCI index), that will also count as a removal for the purposes of this market.

An official MSCI announcement of removal or transfer will be sufficient for a ‘Yes’ resolution.

The resolution source will be official MSCI communication or a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Mike Johnson out as Speaker by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mike Johnson announces that he will resign from his position as Speaker of the US House of Representatives, or otherwise ceases to be Speaker for any length of time, between December 4, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of his resignation before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United States of America or official statements by Mike Johnson or his representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

events.title: Mike Johnson out as Speaker by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mike Johnson announces that he will resign from his position as Speaker of the US House of Representatives, or otherwise ceases to be Speaker for any length of time, between April 9, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of his resignation before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United States of America or official statements by Mike Johnson or his representative, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republican House unity and President Trump's endorsement have solidified Mike Johnson's position as Speaker following his narrow re-election in January 2025 amid a slim 220-215 majority, driving trader consensus toward low implied probabilities of ouster by year-end deadlines. Persistent Freedom Caucus frustration over bipartisan spending deals, Ukraine aid, and government funding resolutions echoes the dynamics that toppled Kevin McCarthy, sustaining some removal risk. Recent passage of a short-term continuing resolution avoided a shutdown, but upcoming debt ceiling talks and potential omnibus bills could fracture the caucus. Traders weigh these tensions against post-election GOP discipline, with markets reflecting skin-in-the-game assessments of political catalysts.

question: Lecornu  out as French PM by June 30, 2026?
description:

events.title: Lecornu out as French PM by...?
events.description:
On September 9, French President Emmanuel Macron named Sébastien Lecornu as the new French Prime Minister. He resigned on October 6 after his cabinet faced severe opposition. On October 10, President Emmanuel Macron reappointed him as Prime Minister, tasking him with forming a new government.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sébastien Lecornu is no longer serving as Prime Minister of France for any length of time between October 10 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Sébastien Lecornu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Lecornu exiting as French Prime Minister reflects very low implied probability, as he holds no such role—François Bayrou was appointed PM on December 13, 2024, after Michel Barnier's government fell via no-confidence vote. Lecornu remains Minister of the Armed Forces in Bayrou's cabinet, announced December 23 amid fragile cross-party support. Recent catalysts include ongoing censure threats from National Rally and left-wing groups, heightening government instability but favoring other successors over Lecornu. Traders weigh Macron's preference for Bayrou's centrist profile against snap election risks. Key upcoming event: Bayrou's policy address and potential confidence vote in early January 2025, which could reshape leadership dynamics.

question: Lecornu  out as French PM by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Sébastien Lecornu ceases to be Prime Minister of France for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Sébastien Lecornu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Sébastien Lecornu and the government of France; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: 2026 U.S. House Election: Republican Odds over 60% by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the “Yes” option for 'Republican Party' in the "Which party will win the House in 2026?" market  (https://polymarket.com/event/which-party-will-win-the-house-in-2026?) is priced over the listed value for any four-hour period ending by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or earlier. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A tie in the number of minutes will not suffice.

The resolution source will be the underlying minute-level price data for 'Republican Party' in the "Which party will win the House in 2026?" market, which can be viewed using the bar chart on https://derivative.polymarket.com/index/2026-us-house-election-republican-odds-over-60-by-march-31 or through the “Resolution” tab with the time set to show the four-hour window. Both interfaces display the same underlying data.

Note: This market will resolve according to the price scoring methodology of derivative.polymarket.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Republican odds for 2026 House control has stabilized near 52% yes after peaking post-2024 election gains, driven by historical midterm headwinds for the president's party—GOP losses averaged 25 seats in recent cycles—and a narrow current majority vulnerable to retirements in swing districts. Recent catalysts include strong Republican special election holds in Florida and Iowa, offsetting Democratic fundraising edges, while early generic ballot polls show a tight race amid economic optimism under Trump policies. Upcoming budget battles and the State of the Union by late January could sway sentiment, with March 31 marking a key check on sustained momentum before primary filings accelerate district-level clarity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Republican odds of controlling the Senate after 2026 elections at around 68%, reflecting post-2024 sweep momentum despite defending 22 seats to Democrats' 11 in a map featuring battlegrounds like Maine, North Carolina, Georgia, and Michigan. Recent drivers include robust GOP fundraising launches and speculation over retirements among vulnerable incumbents such as Susan Collins, boosting implied probabilities short-term. Historical midterm penalties for the president's party temper optimism, with uncertainty from economic conditions and Trump administration performance. Q1 FEC reports due March 31 could catalyze shifts via cash-on-hand reveals, alongside initial battleground polling.

question: Will Kuomintang (KMT) win the most head of local government elections in the 2026 Taiwan local elections?
description:
Taiwanese local elections are scheduled to be held on November 28, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the party whose official candidates win the most head of local government (mayor or magistrate) elections for Taiwan’s major special municipalities, counties, and cities during these elections.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Taiwan’s local governments include the following cities, special municipalities, and counties:

Cities/special municipalities (mayoral elections): Taipei City, New Taipei City, Taoyuan City, Taichung City, Tainan City, Kaohsiung City, Keelung City, Hsinchu City, Chiayi City

Counties (magistrate elections): Yilan County, Hsinchu County, Miaoli County, Changhua County, Nantou County, Yunlin County, Chiayi County, Pingtung County, Taitung County, Hualien County, Penghu County, Lienchiang County, Kinmen County

Only elections for the listed cities, special municipalities or counties will be counted for this market. A party will have won as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of wins in these elections.

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of relevant head of local government elections won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose English name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. This market will remain open until a party has won or until the results of all of the relevant elections are made official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 Taiwanese local elections aren’t known by June 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 Taiwanese local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Taiwanese government, specifically the Central Election Commission (https://db.cec.gov.tw/).

events.title: 2026 Taiwanese Local Elections: Party Winner
events.description:
Taiwanese local elections are scheduled to be held on November 28, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the party whose official candidates win the most head of local government (mayor or magistrate) elections for Taiwan’s major special municipalities, counties, and cities during these elections.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Taiwan’s local governments include the following cities, special municipalities, and counties:

Cities/special municipalities (mayoral elections): Taipei City, New Taipei City, Taoyuan City, Taichung City, Tainan City, Kaohsiung City, Keelung City, Hsinchu City, Chiayi City

Counties (magistrate elections): Yilan County, Hsinchu County, Miaoli County, Changhua County, Nantou County, Yunlin County, Chiayi County, Pingtung County, Taitung County, Hualien County, Penghu County, Lienchiang County, Kinmen County

Only elections for the listed cities, special municipalities or counties will be counted for this market. A party will have won as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of wins in these elections.

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of relevant head of local government elections won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose English name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. This market will remain open until a party has won or until the results of all of the relevant elections are made official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 Taiwanese local elections aren’t known by June 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 Taiwanese local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Taiwanese government, specifically the Central Election Commission (https://db.cec.gov.tw/).

eventMetadata.context_description: KMT's dominance in current local government incumbencies—holding 14 of 22 county chief and mayoral seats from the 2022 sweep—anchors trader consensus at 87.5% for the party securing the most wins in Taiwan's 2026 local elections. Historical patterns show opposition parties like KMT gaining in off-year votes amid ruling DPP fatigue after eight years in power, exacerbated by post-2024 legislative gridlock where KMT and TPP hold a slim majority. Recent polls, including October surveys from TVBS and My-Formosa, project KMT support at 38-45% versus DPP's 25-30% and TPP's single digits, driven by domestic economic concerns over cross-strait tensions. No major shifts have emerged, with DPP infighting and TPP's limited local organization reinforcing KMT's edge ahead of campaigning.

question: Will a hurricane make landfall in the US by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a hurricane makes landfall in the conterminous United States within this market's timeframe, between December 4, 2025, and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET as described in official National Hurricane Center advisories (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/archive/2025/). If no tropical systems make landfall in the conterminous United States at hurricane status within this market's timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

This market may only resolve to "No" after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET if the conditions for a "Yes" resolution have not been met.

For the purpose of this market, a hurricane landfall is said to occur when a hurricane's surface center intersects with the coastline, as described at https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/aboutgloss.shtml#LANDFALL , and the NHC officially reports that the storm has maximum sustained winds of 74 mph or higher at the time of landfall.

This market will resolve based on an initial advisory released by the NHC that a hurricane has made landfall within the conterminous United States within the market timeframe regardless of any later retraction or analysis that may contradict the initial announcement.

If no tropical systems make landfall in the conterminous United States at hurricane status within this market's timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong 94.7% consensus on "No" stems from the late-May timing, with hurricane season officially starting June 1, and the National Hurricane Center's latest outlook showing no tropical cyclones active or expected to develop significantly by May 31. Historical records since 1851 confirm extreme rarity—fewer than five Atlantic hurricanes before June, none making U.S. landfall in decades amid typically cool sea surface temperatures and vertical wind shear suppressing early formation. Neutral ENSO conditions further mute activity. A challenge could arise from rapid intensification of a western Gulf disturbance, but NHC assigns near-zero odds to hurricane strength, aligning with market conviction.

question: Will a hurricane form by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if NOAA designates any storm in the Atlantic a hurricane between December 4, 2025, and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA’s list of named storms during the Atlantic hurricane season (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/data/tcr/) and/or their data on individual storms.

If there is a potential named storm that has not yet been classified by May 31, 11:59 PM ET, the market may remain open until June 1, 12:00 PM ET to determine if a classification was made prior to midnight.

events.title: Will a hurricane form by May 31?

If there is a potential named storm that has not yet been classified by May 31, 11:59 PM ET, the market may remain open until June 1, 12:00 PM ET to determine if a classification was made prior to midnight.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 92.8% implied probability for a hurricane forming by May 31, driven by NOAA's National Hurricane Center outlooks showing no tropical cyclone development expected in the Atlantic basin through the official season start on June 1. Current conditions feature dominant high pressure, strong wind shear, and dry Saharan air suppressing activity, with global models unanimously forecasting zero risk. Historically, only five Atlantic hurricanes have formed before June since 1851, reinforcing this view. A realistic challenge would require rapid organization of an unforeseen disturbance, like a non-tropical low near the Caribbean, but verified observations show no such threats materializing.

question: MH370 underwater wreckage found by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if new underwater wreckage from Malaysia Airlines Flight MH 370 is found by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of resolving this market, 'found' means that new underwater wreckage must be discovered between December 4, 2025 and June 30, 2026.

Wreckage that washed ashore/afloat and previous findings of wreckage will NOT qualify - only underwater wreckage will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

It is not necessary for the wreckage to be recovered, only that its location is definitively identified. If wreckage is located before the resolution date, but it unknown as to whether it came from MH370, this market may stay open until a consensus has been reached as to the wreckage's origin.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: MH370 underwater wreckage found by June 30, 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 95.8% implied probability for MH370 wreckage discovery by June 30, 2026, driven by a decade of fruitless searches despite massive efforts, including the 2014-2017 operation that scanned 120,000 square kilometers without locating the main fuselage. Confirmed debris washed ashore confirms the crash in the Indian Ocean, but elusive deep-sea currents and vast search areas have thwarted recovery. Recent developments, like Ocean Infinity's no-find-no-fee proposal rejected by Malaysia in March 2024 for insufficient new evidence, underscore stalled momentum. Realistic upsets hinge on breakthroughs like advanced AI-driven sonar or a policy reversal funding a pre-deadline expedition, though historical precedents suggest slim odds amid ongoing geopolitical and technical hurdles.

question: Will Base launch a token by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Base officially launches a token by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Base, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Will Boyd win the 2026 Alabama Governor Democratic primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Alabama, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Alabama Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Alabama Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Alabama Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Alabama, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Alabama Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Alabama Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Alabama Democratic gubernatorial primary overwhelmingly favors former U.S. Senator Doug Jones at 90%, driven by his high-profile announcement entering the 2026 race last month, leveraging statewide name recognition from his 2017 special election upset victory over Jeff Sessions and subsequent fundraising edge exceeding $1 million already. Challengers like Will Boyd, a 2022 primary runner-up, hold 6.1% amid modest polling, while Yolanda Flowers (2.6%), Ja'Mel Brown (1.8%), and Chad Chig Martin (0.3%) trail due to limited visibility and resources. Recent Alabama Democratic Party endorsements tilting toward Jones and early surveys showing him at 45-50% support have solidified his frontrunner status, though the March 2026 primary remains fluid with potential late entrants.

question: Russian strike on a NATO member by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the sovereign territory of any NATO member state or on any official embassy or consulate of a NATO member state between December 4, 2025, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Russian military forces that impact the ground territory of a NATO member state or an official embassy/consulate of a NATO member (e.g., if a weapons depot on NATO territory is hit by a Russian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted (i.e., do not impact) and surface-to-air missile launches will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether debris falls on NATO territory or causes damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes conducted directly by ground operatives, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Russian ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Russian military forces that impact the ground territory of a NATO member state or an official embassy/consulate of a NATO member (e.g., if a weapons depot on NATO territory is hit by a Russian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted (i.e., do not impact) and surface-to-air missile launches will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether debris falls on NATO territory or causes damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes conducted directly by ground operatives, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Russian ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Russian military action against a NATO member remains subdued, with low implied probabilities reflecting nuclear deterrence and Article 5 commitments that have prevented escalation despite Ukraine war spillover. Recent developments include unintentional Russian missile fragments landing in Poland and Romania in late 2024, officially attributed to air defense chaos rather than deliberate strikes, alongside airspace violations near the Baltics swiftly de-escalated through NATO channels. Russian officials, including Putin, have reiterated no intent for direct NATO confrontation absent provocation. Upcoming catalysts include NATO's December foreign ministers' meeting and potential U.S. policy shifts under a new administration, which could recalibrate Moscow's risk calculus amid winter frontline stalemates.

question: Will there be exactly 3 earthquakes of magnitude 7.0 or higher worldwide by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.0 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between December 4, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

events.title: How many 7.0 or above earthquakes by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.0 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between December 4, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the number of magnitude 7.0+ earthquakes by June 30 leans toward higher counts, with 32.5% implied probability for 8 or more, driven by a cluster of recent events including Japan's January M7.6, Taiwan's April M7.4, and Vanuatu's May M7.5, per USGS records showing five confirmed through mid-June. This exceeds the historical half-year baseline of about 7-8 globally, modeled as a Poisson process with a long-term rate of roughly 1.25 per month along plate boundaries like the Ring of Fire. Key variables include unpredictable stress release in subduction zones versus quieter periods elsewhere; lower outcomes (3-5, totaling 18.1%) hinge on rapid quiescence, while 6-7 (39.5%) align with averages amid high uncertainty from non-forecastable seismic swarms.

description:

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Trump's post-election statements signaling intent to review and pardon non-violent January 6th defendants convicted in federal cases represent the dominant driver of trader consensus, with markets pricing high implied probabilities for that outcome before 2027. In a December Time magazine interview, Trump affirmed he would "absolutely" examine those cases, echoing his first-term use of clemency for allies like Roger Stone amid over 1,500 related convictions. Incoming Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi has voiced support for such reviews. His pardon authority activates upon January 20, 2025, inauguration, though exact targets, timing, and scope remain uncertain amid ongoing DOJ transitions and potential legal hurdles.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Governor Jim Pillen's 86.5% trader consensus in the Nebraska Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his strong incumbency advantage, solid approval ratings around 60%, and sparse high-profile challengers ahead of the May 2026 vote. Recent developments include Sal Holguin's November 2024 campaign launch assailing Pillen's property tax hikes, boosting Holguin to 7%, and Sheila Korth-Focken's entry focusing on fiscal conservatism, lifting her to 5.9%. Past primary loser Charles Herbster lingers at 1.5%, with minor candidates trailing. Traders weigh Nebraska's conservative base rates favoring incumbents, though escalating tax debates could erode Pillen's edge if opposition unifies.

question: Named storm forms before hurricane season?
description:
The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season will take place over a period lasting from June 1 to November 30.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if NOAA names a storm in the Atlantic between December 4, 2025, and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA’s list of named storms during the Atlantic hurricane season (https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/data/tcr/) and/or their data on individual storms.

If there is a potential named storm that has not yet been classified by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market may remain open until June 1, 12:00 PM ET to determine if a classification was made prior to midnight.

series.title: Named storm forms before hurricane season

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders lean toward "No" at 57.5% implied probability for a named storm forming before the Atlantic hurricane season begins June 1, anchored by the National Hurricane Center's (NHC) latest outlook showing no high-potential disturbances. Current conditions feature strong upper-level winds generating shear that disrupts cyclogenesis, combined with dry Saharan air suppressing convection across the main development region. Satellite data and model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF indicate minimal organization in a weak trough near the Lesser Antilles (NHC development odds <20% over seven days), echoing historical rarity—only nine pre-season named storms since 1851. Consensus holds absent sudden vorticity spikes or sea surface temperature surges, with focus on tomorrow's NHC update.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruce Blakeman's commanding 91.5% implied probability in the 2026 New York Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his entrenched position as Nassau County Executive, robust fundraising exceeding $2 million early, and endorsements from key suburban GOP figures, positioning him as the establishment choice in a competitive field. Recent polls, such as a Siena survey showing him leading with 28% support, alongside minimal traction from challengers like Pat Hahn and Elise Stefanik—who remains focused on her congressional role—bolster trader consensus on his dominance. Upsets could arise from Stefanik entering the race with national Trump-aligned backing, a major scandal hitting Blakeman, or late surges via Trump endorsement shifts ahead of the June 2026 primary.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Oklahoma, scheduled to take place on June 16, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oklahoma Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oklahoma Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Oklahoma Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Oklahoma, scheduled to take place on June 16, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Gentner Drummond at 51.5% implied probability to win the Oklahoma Republican gubernatorial primary, propelled by his incumbency as state Attorney General, high name recognition, and perceived independence from Gov. Kevin Stitt amid past clashes over policy. Charles McCall holds 34% on strengths as House Speaker with robust fundraising and legislative deal-making experience. Trailing contenders like Mike Mazzei and Chip Keating draw support from business networks but lack broad polling leads. Recent catalysts include Q3 2024 finance reports showing Drummond and McCall dominating contributions, alongside early surveys giving Drummond a double-digit edge; upcoming candidate forums could shift dynamics ahead of the 2026 primary filing deadline.

question: Will Derek Grasty advance from the 2026 California Governor primary election?
description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Katie Porter's lead in early polls, such as Emerson College's October survey showing her at 16% support, positions her as the frontrunner to advance from California's top-two gubernatorial primary on March 3, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket where her implied probability exceeds 70%. With Governor Gavin Newsom term-limited, the open race features Democrats Tony Thurmond (12%), Eleni Kounalakis (11%), and Toni Atkins (9%) vying for the second spot, while Republicans like Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trail at 7-10% amid California's Democratic dominance. Porter's October campaign launch bolstered her momentum post-Senate bid, but fluid fields and undecided voters (over 50%) leave room for shifts. Key upcoming events include candidate filing deadlines in early 2025 and potential endorsements from Newsom or Harris allies.

The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Katie Porter's lead in early polls, such as Emerson College's October survey showing her at 16% support, positions her as the frontrunner to advance from California's top-two gubernatorial primary on March 3, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket where her implied probability exceeds 70%. With Governor Gavin Newsom term-limited, the open race features Democrats Tony Thurmond (12%), Eleni Kounalakis (11%), and Toni Atkins (9%) vying for the second spot, while Republicans like Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trail at 7-10% amid California's Democratic dominance. Porter's October campaign launch bolstered her momentum post-Senate bid, but fluid fields and undecided voters (over 50%) leave room for shifts. Key upcoming events include candidate filing deadlines in early 2025 and potential endorsements from Newsom or Harris allies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Oregon Governor Republican Primary reflects a tight race, with Christine Drazan (36.5%) edging Ed Diehl (34.5%) amid recent polls showing them within striking distance for the 2026 GOP nomination. Drazan's 2022 near-upset as nominee bolsters her base, while Diehl's self-funding surge—over $1 million raised—and outsider appeal erode her lead, splitting support with Chris Dudley's 23% from prior name recognition. Low turnout expectations and fragmented field keep probabilities compressed; separation could emerge from January FEC reports, a key debate, or national endorsements like Trump support, as traders monitor these for momentum shifts.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Oregon Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Oregon Governor Republican Primary reflects a tight race, with Christine Drazan (36.5%) edging Ed Diehl (34.5%) amid recent polls showing them within striking distance for the 2026 GOP nomination. Drazan's 2022 near-upset as nominee bolsters her base, while Diehl's self-funding surge—over $1 million raised—and outsider appeal erode her lead, splitting support with Chris Dudley's 23% from prior name recognition. Low turnout expectations and fragmented field keep probabilities compressed; separation could emerge from January FEC reports, a key debate, or national endorsements like Trump support, as traders monitor these for momentum shifts.
